fields.label: Hostname
fields.name: hostname
fields.help: The hostname is the unique device name shown in the CLI prompt. It improves documentation, troubleshooting, and syslog analysis and prevents confusion between multiple routers and switches.

fields.label: Domain
fields.name: domain
fields.help: Cisco IOS requires a domain name before generating RSA keys for SSH. Example: example.local or company.local.

fields.label: Enable Secret
fields.name: enableSecret
fields.help: The enable secret protects privileged EXEC mode. It should always be used instead of enable password because it is stored more securely.

fields.label: password min.
fields.name: minPass
fields.help: Sets a minimum length for new passwords. Useful for hardening. Typical values are 10, 12, or higher.

fields.label: no domain lookup
fields.name: noDomainLookup
fields.help: Prevents DNS lookups for mistyped commands. Without this command, the router attempts to resolve typing errors as host names, which causes delays.

fields.label: password encryption
fields.name: servicePass
fields.help: Encrypts simple passwords in the configuration using type 7. This is not strong protection, but it prevents plain-text display of basic passwords.

fields.label: Time zone
fields.name: clockTimezoneName
fields.help: Short name of the standard time zone for the `clock timezone` command, for example CET. The label and UTC offset are configured separately.

fields.label: UTC offset hours
fields.name: clockTimezoneHours
fields.help: UTC offset in hours for standard time. The default for Germany is 1. Negative values such as -5 are supported.

fields.label: UTC offset minutes
fields.name: clockTimezoneMinutes
fields.help: Additional minute offset relative to UTC. Usually 0; use values such as 30 for time zones with half-hour offsets.

fields.label: Daylight saving time name
fields.name: clockSummerName
fields.help: Short name of the daylight-saving time zone for `clock summer-time`, for example CEST. Leave empty when daylight-saving time is not required.

fields.label: Daylight saving time rule
fields.name: clockSummerRule
fields.help: Complete rule section after the daylight-saving-time name. Example: recurring last Sun Mar 2:00 last Sun Oct 3:00. A date-based IOS rule can be entered instead.

fields.label: Company
fields.name: company
fields.help: The {COMPANY} variable is replaced only when it is used in the banner text.

fields.label: Location
fields.name: location
fields.help: Variable {LOCATION}. Useful when multiple sites or labs are involved.

fields.label: Admin / contact
fields.name: admin
fields.help: Variable {ADMIN}. Enter an email address, telephone number, or operations contact.

fields.label: MOTD Banner
fields.name: motd
fields.help: The MOTD banner appears before or during login. Use it for legal notices, warnings, and contact information. Variables: {HOSTNAME}, {DATE}, {TIME}, {DEVICE_TYPE}, {LOCATION}, {ADMIN}, {COMPANY}.

fields.label: Login Banner
fields.name: loginBanner
fields.help: The login banner is displayed when accessing the console or VTY lines. Useful for clear warnings and ownership information.

fields.label: RSA Bits
fields.name: rsaModulus
fields.help: RSA key length for SSH. 2048 bits is the standard value. Packet Tracer commonly accepts 1024 or 2048. Use 2048 bits or higher on real devices.

fields.label: SSH timeout (seconds)
fields.name: sshTimeout
fields.help: Number of seconds an SSH authentication session may remain open.

fields.label: SSH retries
fields.name: sshRetries
fields.help: Maximum number of authentication attempts per SSH connection.

fields.label: SSH source interface
fields.name: sshSourceIf
fields.help: Optional source interface for SSH, for example Vlan99 or Loopback0.

fields.label: SSH logging events
fields.name: sshLogEvents
fields.help: Enables SSH-Event-Logging, if from the IOS supported.

fields.label: SSH Algorithm
fields.name: sshAlgorithm
fields.help: Optional. Configure this only if the target IOS version supports the command.

fields.label: VTY start
fields.name: vtyStart
fields.help: Starting number of the VTY line range. Usually 0.

fields.label: VTY end
fields.name: vtyEnd
fields.help: Last VTY line number. Traditional ranges use 0 4; many devices support 0 15.

fields.label: VTY Login
fields.name: vtyLoginMode
fields.help: `login local` uses local user accounts. `login authentication default` uses AAA. Password login uses a line password.

fields.label: VTY password
fields.name: vtyPassword
fields.help: Relevant only when password login is used. Leave empty otherwise.

fields.label: VTY ACL
fields.name: vtyAclSsh
fields.help: Optional access class that limits SSH source networks, for example 10 or MGMT-SSH.

fields.label: Exec timeout
fields.name: vtyExecTimeout
fields.help: Automatic logout in minutes and seconds, for example 10 0.

fields.label: Logging synchronous
fields.name: vtyLoggingSync
fields.help: `logging synchronous` prevents console messages from interrupting command input.

fields.label: Privilege
fields.name: vtyPrivilege
fields.help: Optional privilege level on VTY lines. Usually leave this empty and manage privileges through local users or AAA.

fields.label: Local AAA
fields.name: aaaLocal
fields.help: Enables aaa new-model with local authentication when no RADIUS or TACACS+ module is active. When RADIUS or TACACS+ is configured, the default method is generated centrally there to avoid conflicting aaa authentication login default commands.

fields.label: Console timeout
fields.name: execTimeout
fields.help: Automatic console logout in minutes and seconds. Example: 10 0 disconnects the session after 10 minutes of inactivity.

fields.label: Console logging synchronous
fields.name: consoleLoggingSync
fields.help: `logging synchronous` prevents console messages from interrupting command input.

fields.label: Console login
fields.name: consoleLogin
fields.help: Optional. login local on the console requires at least one local user. VTY and SSH settings are configured exclusively in the SSH module.

fields.label: RADIUS: Name|IP|Authentication port|Accounting port|Key|Timeout|Retransmit
fields.name: radiusList
fields.help: Multiple RADIUS servers are supported. Authentication normally uses port 1812 and accounting port 1813. Timeout and retransmit values are optional. Example: RAD1|192.168.99.10|1812|1813|Secret|5|3

fields.label: TACACS+: name|IP|Key
fields.name: tacacsList
fields.help: Multiple TACACS+ servers are supported. TACACS+ separates authentication, authorization, and accounting and is well suited to administrative access.

fields.label: AAA RADIUS group
fields.name: aaaRadiusGroup
fields.help: Creates an `aaa group server radius` group and assigns the configured RADIUS servers to it.

fields.label: Use AAA RADIUS
fields.name: aaaUseRadius
fields.help: When enabled, AAA login, authorization, and accounting use RADIUS with a local fallback.

fields.label: Source Interface
fields.name: radiusSourceIf
fields.help: Optional source interface for RADIUS packets, for example Loopback0 or Vlan99. This provides a stable source address for firewalls and server communication.

fields.label: NTP: Server|Prefer
fields.name: ntpList
fields.help: NTP synchronizes device time. Accurate time is important for syslog, certificates, troubleshooting, and event correlation. Format: server IP|optional prefer.

fields.label: Syslog: Server|Level
fields.name: syslogList
fields.help: Syslog sends log messages to a central server. Supported levels include emergencies, alerts, critical, errors, warnings, notifications, informational, and debugging.

fields.label: SNMPv3: group|user|auth|authpass|priv|privpass
fields.name: snmpList
fields.help: SNMPv3 is significantly more secure for monitoring than SNMPv2 because it supports authentication and encryption. Example: MON|snmpuser|sha|authpass|aes|privpass.

fields.label: Timestamp settings
fields.name: logTimestamps
fields.help: Enables detailed timestamps for log and debug messages.

fields.label: Buffered size
fields.name: logBuffered
fields.help: size the local Logpuffers.

fields.label: Trap level
fields.name: logTrap
fields.help: Syslog trap level.

fields.label: Archive configuration changes
fields.name: archiveLog
fields.help: Logs configuration changes.

fields.label: Login auditing
fields.name: loginAudit
fields.help: Logs successful and failed login attempts.

fields.label: Archive path
fields.name: archivePath
fields.help: Optional destination for configuration archives, for example flash:/archive-$h or scp://user:pass@server/path. This uses the same archive configuration mode as configuration logging.

fields.label: Archive on write memory
fields.name: archiveWriteMemory
fields.help: Archives the configuration for write memory, if from the IOS supported.

fields.label: Archive time period
fields.name: archiveTimePeriod
fields.help: Optional archive interval in minutes. Leave empty when configurations should be archived only manually.

fields.label: User|Privilege|Secret
fields.name: privUserList
fields.help: Central management of local users. This list prevents duplicate username commands. Privilege level 15 grants full administrative access; lower levels provide restricted access.

fields.label: Parser view|Secret|Command
fields.name: parserViewList
fields.help: Optional parser views for restricted CLI permissions. Support depends on the IOS or IOS-XE platform. Use them when users require selected commands without full administrative privileges.

fields.label: Interface|Description|Routed/Access/Trunk|IP/VLAN/Allowed VLANs|Mask
fields.name: interfacesList
fields.help: routed = Layer 3 port with an IP address. access = endpoint port in one VLAN. trunk = connection between switches or to a router or firewall carrying multiple VLANs.

fields.label: Range|description|access/trunk|VLANs|PortFast|BPDU Guard|PortSecurity
fields.name: rangesList
fields.help: Configures multiple ports at once. Use PortFast only on end-device ports. BPDU Guard disables a port when an unexpected switch or BPDU is detected. Port security limits learned MAC addresses.

fields.label: Interface|Sub-ID|VLAN-ID|IP|mask
fields.name: subifList
fields.help: Router-on-a-stick uses one physical interface for multiple VLANs through 802.1Q tags. The subinterface ID commonly matches the VLAN ID, but this is not technically required.

fields.label: Parent interface
fields.name: rosParentIf
fields.help: Physical router interface used as the trunk toward the switch. The connected switch port must also be configured as a trunk.

fields.label: Optional native VLAN
fields.name: rosNativeVlan
fields.help: Optional native VLAN. Configure it only when untagged traffic is intentionally required. Usually leave it empty or use a dedicated unused VLAN.

fields.label: Enable parent interface
fields.name: rosParentNoShutdown
fields.help: Applies `no shutdown` to the physical parent interface. IP addresses are configured on the subinterfaces.

fields.label: VLANs/Subinterfaces: VLAN ID|Name|Gateway IP|Mask
fields.name: rosVlanList
fields.help: One line per VLAN or subinterface. The module generates router subinterfaces with `interface Parent.VLAN`, `encapsulation dot1Q`, `ip address`, and `no shutdown`. The VLANs must already exist on the switch.

fields.label: Trust interfaces
fields.name: qosTrustInterfaces
fields.help: Enables DSCP Trust / Auto-QoS Trust on Interfaces. platform-dependent.

fields.label: Class|DSCP|Policy|Priority percentage
fields.name: qosPolicyList
fields.help: Generates simple class-map/policy-map Struktur.

fields.label: Interface|Profile|Description|Value1|Value2
fields.name: interfaceProfileList
fields.value:
Gi0/1|access|Client port|10|
Gi0/2|voice|Phone and client|10|30
Gi0/48|trunk|Uplink|10,20,30,99|999
Gi0/10|unused|Not used|999|
fields.help: Profiles: access, voice, trunk, uplink, server, AP, unused, and routed. Value1 and Value2 contain VLANs, a native VLAN, or an IP address and mask depending on the selected profile.

fields.label: Interface|Allowed VLANs|Native VLAN|Description|Guard(root/loop)
fields.name: trunkUplinkList
fields.help: Generates a clean trunk or uplink configuration with `nonegotiate` and optional Root Guard or Loop Guard.

fields.label: Provider VLAN name prefix
fields.name: qinqVlanPrefix
fields.help: Prefix used for automatically generated provider or service VLAN names. Example: QINQ_SVLAN_200.

fields.label: Optional system MTU
fields.name: qinqSystemMtu
fields.help: Optional and platform-dependent. QinQ adds four bytes for each additional 802.1Q tag. Leave this field empty unless the target platform and the complete path require a larger MTU. Many Catalyst platforms require a reload after changing the system MTU.

fields.label: Global QinQ option
fields.name: qinqGlobalDot1q
fields.help: Informational option only. Most Catalyst configurations enable QinQ per port with switchport mode dot1q-tunnel. Global Ethertype commands vary by platform and are therefore not generated automatically.

fields.label: Interface|Provider VLAN|Description|L2 protocol tunnel
fields.name: qinqTunnelList
fields.value:
Gi0/10|200|Kunde A QinQ|cdp,stp,vtp
Gi0/11|201|Kunde B QinQ|stp
fields.help: Creates customer-facing QinQ tunnel ports. Each line configures dot1q-tunnel mode, assigns the outer provider VLAN, and can optionally tunnel CDP, STP, or VTP. Format: Interface|Provider VLAN|Description|L2 protocol tunnel.

fields.label: L3 switch only: Parent|Sub-ID|Provider VLAN|Customer VLAN|Optional IP|Optional mask|Description
fields.name: qinqSubifList
fields.help: Creates double-tagged Layer 3 subinterfaces on supported Layer 3 switches. The encapsulation uses an outer provider VLAN and an inner customer VLAN. Optional IPv4 addressing can terminate the customer service on the subinterface.

fields.label: Load balancing
fields.name: etherLoadBalance
fields.help: Optional port-channel load-balancing method. Availability depends on the target platform.

fields.label: Port Channel|Interfaces|Mode|Access/Trunk|VLANs|Description|Native VLAN
fields.name: etherExtList
fields.help: Supports LACP active/passive, PAgP desirable/auto, or static on mode. Generates the member-interface range and the Port Channel interface.

fields.label: Interface|Role|Description|Access VLAN|Voice VLAN|Allowed VLANs|Native VLAN|Port channel|IP|Mask|VRF
fields.name: interfaceRoleList
fields.value:
Gi0/1|access|Client|10||||||
Gi0/2|voice|Phone|10|30|||||
Gi0/48|uplink|Core uplink|||10,20,30,99|999||||
Gi1/0/1|pc-member|Bundle member|||||1|||
Gi0/10|unused|Not used|999||||||
fields.help: Central role-based interface model. Supported roles include access, voice, trunk, uplink, AP, port-channel member, routed, and unused. The generator derives the appropriate switchport, spanning-tree, channel-group, routing, shutdown, and VRF commands from each role.

fields.label: VLANs: VLAN ID|Name
fields.name: vlanList
fields.help: VLANs separate Layer 2 broadcast domains. VLAN 1 should not be used as a production or management VLAN.

fields.label: SVIs: VLAN ID|IP|Mask
fields.name: sviList
fields.help: An SVI is a Switch Virtual Interface. On a Layer 2 switch it provides the management IP. On a Layer 3 switch it commonly acts as the default gateway for clients in the VLAN.

fields.label: Default gateway L2
fields.name: defaultGateway
fields.help: Relevant only to Layer 2 switches. It allows the switch to reach management networks outside its local subnet, for example for SSH access from other networks.

fields.label: Data VLAN
fields.name: voipDataVlan
fields.help: Data VLAN for clients connected behind an IP phone. It is created as a normal VLAN and applied as the access VLAN on phone ports.

fields.label: Data VLAN name
fields.name: voipDataVlanName
fields.help: Name of the data VLAN. A clear name improves show command output and documentation.

fields.label: Voice VLAN
fields.name: voipVoiceVlan
fields.help: Voice VLAN for IP phones. It is created as a VLAN and applied to phone ports with switchport voice vlan.

fields.label: Voice VLAN name
fields.name: voipVoiceVlanName
fields.help: Name of the voice VLAN. Use a short and unambiguous value such as VOICE, VOIP, or TELEPHONY.

fields.label: Enable CDP
fields.name: voipCdp
fields.help: CDP allows Cisco IP phones to discover the configured voice VLAN automatically.

fields.label: Enable LLDP
fields.name: voipLldp
fields.help: LLDP is vendor-neutral. Many phones use LLDP-MED to discover the voice VLAN and network policy.

fields.label: QoS mode
fields.name: voipQosMode
fields.help: Selects QoS handling for phone ports. AutoQoS creates a Cisco phone profile; DSCP or CoS trust modes preserve existing markings.

fields.label: DHCP pool name
fields.name: voipDhcpPool
fields.help: Name of the DHCP pool for phones. The pool is generated only when network, mask, and gateway values are present.

fields.label: Voice network
fields.name: voipNetwork
fields.help: Network address of the voice VLAN used by the DHCP pool.

fields.label: Voice subnet mask
fields.name: voipMask
fields.help: Subnet mask of the voice network used by the DHCP pool.

fields.label: Voice gateway
fields.name: voipGateway
fields.help: Default gateway for phones, normally the SVI or routed-interface address of the voice VLAN.

fields.label: Optional DNS server
fields.name: voipDns
fields.help: Optional DNS server distributed to phones through DHCP. Leave empty when DNS should not be supplied by this pool.

fields.label: Optional TFTP / CUCM server
fields.name: voipTftp
fields.help: Optional TFTP or CallManager address. Cisco phones commonly receive it through DHCP option 150.

fields.label: Optional excluded range
fields.name: voipExcluded
fields.help: Optional DHCP exclusion. Enter a start and end address or a single address. Leave empty when no addresses should be excluded.

fields.label: Interface|Description|Access VLAN|Voice VLAN|PortFast|BPDU Guard|QoS|Port security
fields.name: voipInterfaceList
fields.value:
Gi0/2|Phone + client|10|30|Yes|Yes|auto qos voip cisco-phone|No
Gi0/3|Office phone|10|30|Yes|Yes|mls qos trust dscp|Yes
fields.help: One line per phone port. The generator configures access and voice VLANs, PortFast, BPDU Guard, optional QoS, and optional port security. This module is available only for Layer 2 and Layer 3 switches.

fields.label: DHCP Snooping VLANs
fields.name: dhcpSnoopVlans
fields.help: DHCP snooping protects against unauthorized DHCP servers. Enter the VLANs in which clients use DHCP, for example 10,20,99.

fields.label: DAI VLANs
fields.name: daiVlans
fields.help: Dynamic ARP Inspection protects against ARP spoofing. It requires a reliable DHCP-snooping binding table or static bindings.

fields.label: Range|Description|Access VLAN|Voice VLAN|PortFast|BPDU Guard|Port Security|Storm Level|Shutdown|Nonegotiate
fields.name: accessBaseList
fields.help: Generates common access-port settings: PortFast, BPDU Guard, port security, storm control, and shutdown for unused ports.

fields.label: Interval in seconds
fields.name: errdisableInterval
fields.help: Delay in seconds before automatic recovery attempts to re-enable an err-disabled interface.

fields.label: Causes, one per line
fields.name: errdisableCauseList
fields.help: Common causes include bpduguard, psecure-violation, udld, link-flap, and storm-control.

fields.label: Spanning-tree mode
fields.name: stpGlobalMode
fields.help: Selects the active Spanning Tree protocol. The interface automatically displays only the options that apply to the selected mode.

fields.label: Platform profile
fields.name: stpPlatformProfile
fields.help: Controls compatibility warnings. The tool generates Cisco IOS/IOS-XE syntax; Packet Tracer may support advanced commands only partially.

fields.label: Optional switch model
fields.name: stpSwitchModel
fields.help: Optional model name for documentation and compatibility checks, for example C9200L-24P-4G.

fields.label: Optional IOS / IOS-XE version
fields.name: stpIosVersion
fields.help: Optional version information, for example 17.12.4. It is used for guidance but does not independently change the target platform.

fields.label: Strict compatibility
fields.name: stpStrictCompatibility
fields.help: When enabled, settings that do not match the selected spanning-tree protocol are excluded from export and reported as warnings.

fields.label: Extend system ID
fields.name: stpExtendSystemId
fields.help: Generates spanning-tree extend system-id.

fields.label: Path cost method
fields.name: stpPathcostMethod
fields.help: Selects the short or long STP path-cost scale.

fields.label: Default PortFast
fields.name: stpPortfastDefault
fields.help: Enables PortFast globally for suitable access ports.

fields.label: Default BPDU Guard
fields.name: stpBpduguardDefault
fields.help: Enables BPDU Guard globally for PortFast-ports.

fields.label: Default BPDU Filter
fields.name: stpBpdufilterDefault
fields.help: Enables BPDU Filter globally. Use it only deliberately because it can suppress BPDUs.

fields.label: Default Loop Guard
fields.name: stpLoopguardDefault
fields.help: Enables Loop Guard globally.

fields.label: EtherChannel guard
fields.name: stpEtherchannelGuard
fields.help: Detects incorrect configured EtherChannels.

fields.label: UplinkFast (legacy PVST)
fields.name: stpUplinkFast
fields.help: Legacy PVST convergence optimization. It is not exported for Rapid-PVST or MST.

fields.label: BackboneFast (legacy PVST)
fields.name: stpBackboneFast
fields.help: Legacy PVST optimization for indirect link failures. Do not export it for Rapid-PVST or MST.

fields.label: VLAN/Range for assistant
fields.name: stpVlanTarget
fields.help: A VLAN or VLAN range, for example 10 or 10,20,30-40.

fields.label: Root role
fields.name: stpVlanRootRole
fields.help: Quick assistant for Root Primary, Root Secondary, or a manual bridge priority.

fields.label: Bridge priority
fields.name: stpVlanPriorityValue
fields.help: Only valid Cisco values in increments of 4096 can be selected.

fields.label: Multiple root roles: VLAN|primary/secondary
fields.name: stpRootList
fields.help: Example: 10|primary. One root role is generated per line.

fields.label: Multiple VLAN priorities: VLAN|Priority
fields.name: stpVlanPriorityList
fields.help: Example: 20|8192. Valid values range from 0 to 61440 in increments of 4096.

fields.label: VLAN timers: VLAN|Hello|Forward delay|Max age
fields.name: stpVlanTimersList
fields.help: Empty columns are omitted. Example: 10|2|15|20.

fields.label: VLAN interface parameters: Interface|VLANs|Port priority|Cost
fields.name: stpVlanInterfaceList
fields.help: Example: Gi1/0/1|10|64|10000. Port priority ranges from 0 to 240 in increments of 16.

fields.label: Interface profiles: Interface|Profile|VLANs|Native VLAN
fields.name: stpInterfaceProfileList
fields.help: Profiles: Client, Server, Access Point, Switch Uplink, Port Channel, and Provider Uplink. VLAN columns are used for documentation and plausibility checks.

fields.label: Interface|PortFast|BPDU Guard|BPDU Filter|Guard|Link type|Port priority|Cost
fields.name: stpInterfaceList
fields.help: PortFast values: Yes, trunk, or No. Guard values: root, loop, or empty. Link type is exported only for Rapid-PVST or MST.

fields.label: Root Guard interfaces
fields.name: stpRootGuardIfs
fields.help: One interface per line. Root Guard prevents a superior root bridge from being elected through this port.

fields.label: Loop Guard interfaces
fields.name: stpLoopGuardIfs
fields.help: One interface per line. Loop Guard protects against unidirectional Spanning Tree failures.

fields.label: MST region name
fields.name: stpMstName
fields.help: The name and revision must be identical on all switches in the same MST region.

fields.label: MST revision
fields.name: stpMstRevision
fields.help: Revision number from 0 to 65535. It must be identical throughout an MST region.

fields.label: MST Max hops
fields.name: stpMstMaxHops
fields.help: Generates spanning-tree mst max-hops. The usual valid range is 1 through 40.

fields.label: MST-instance for assistant
fields.name: stpMstTargetInstance
fields.help: Instances range from 1 to 4094. Instance 0 is the IST/CIST and is not used for a separate VLAN mapping.

fields.label: MST root role
fields.name: stpMstRootRole
fields.help: Quick assistant for a root role or a manual instance priority.

fields.label: MST bridge priority
fields.name: stpMstPriorityValue
fields.help: Only valid values in increments of 4096.

fields.label: MST: Instance|VLANs|Priority|Root role
fields.name: stpMstInstanceList
fields.help: Example: 1|10,20|4096| or 2|30-40||primary.

fields.label: MST interface parameters: Interface|Instance|Port priority|Cost
fields.name: stpMstInterfaceList
fields.help: Example: Port-channel1|1|64|20000.

fields.label: MST region comparison: Switch|Name|Revision|Instance:VLANs;...|Optional digest
fields.name: stpMstPeerRegionList
fields.help: Compares other switches with the local region. Example: SW2|COMPANY|1|1:10,20;2:30-40|ABCD.

fields.label: BPDU Guard errdisable recovery
fields.name: stpErrdisableBpduguard
fields.help: Generates errdisable recovery cause bpduguard.

fields.label: Errdisable interval in seconds
fields.name: stpErrdisableInterval
fields.help: Generated together with BPDU Guard recovery. A typical value is 300 seconds.

fields.label: Reset: scope|target|parameter
fields.name: stpResetList
fields.help: Scopes: global, VLAN, MST, and interface. Examples: VLAN|10|priority or interface|Gi1/0/1|portfast.

fields.label: Live-STP-preview
fields.name: stpLivePreview
fields.help: Displays only the currently generated STP configuration section. Hidden fields are not exported when they do not apply to the selected mode.

name: switchSecExt
title: DHCP Snooping / DAI / IP Source Guard

fields.label: DHCP Snooping VLANs
fields.name: sseDhcpVlans
fields.help: VLANs for DHCP Snooping.

fields.label: DAI VLANs
fields.name: sseDaiVlans
fields.help: VLANs for Dynamic ARP Inspection.

fields.label: Trusted interfaces
fields.name: sseTrustedIfs
fields.help: Uplink or DHCP-server-facing ports that require DHCP Snooping and Dynamic ARP Inspection trust.

fields.label: Rate limit: Interface|Rate
fields.name: sseRateLimitIfs
fields.help: Configures a DHCP Snooping rate limit per interface.

fields.label: IP Source Guard interfaces
fields.name: sseSourceGuardIfs
fields.help: Enables `ip verify source` on each listed interface.

fields.label: Interface|Maximum|Sticky yes/no|Violation|Aging time|Aging type
fields.name: portSecExtList
fields.help: Advanced Port Security settings per interface.

fields.label: VLAN|Name|IPv4 network|Mask|Gateway|IPv6 gateway|VRF|DHCP yes/no|DNS|Helper|HSRP VIP|OSPF area|BGP yes/no
fields.name: vlanIpPlanList
fields.help: Maintains a central VLAN and IP plan. Each line can create the VLAN, SVI, optional VRF assignment, IPv4 and IPv6 gateway, helper address, HSRP virtual IP, and DHCP pool. OSPF and BGP values are included as planning information.

fields.label: IPv4 routing
fields.name: ipRouting
fields.help: Enables IPv4 routing with `ip routing` on a Layer 3 switch. Routing is usually already active on routers, but the command remains valid and can still be generated.

fields.label: Destination|Mask|Next hop / interface|Optional AD
fields.name: staticRouteList
fields.help: Static routes define fixed paths. The administrative distance is optional; a higher value can create a floating static route that is used only when a dynamic or lower-distance route fails.

fields.label: Process ID
fields.name: ospfPid
fields.help: The OSPF process ID is locally significant on this router and does not need to match neighboring routers. It identifies the local OSPF process.

fields.label: configuration method
fields.name: ospfConfigMode
fields.help: Process-based mode generates `network ... area ...` statements in the OSPF process. Interface-based mode generates `ip ospf <process> area <area>` under interfaces. Mixed mode allows both approaches.

fields.label: Router ID
fields.name: ospfRid
fields.help: Unique 32-bit identifier in IPv4 format, for example 1.1.1.1. It identifies the OSPF router in LSAs, adjacencies, and the link-state database and must be unique within the OSPF domain.

fields.label: Reference bandwidth
fields.name: ospfRef
fields.help: Reference bandwidth in Mbit/s used for OSPF cost calculation. Example: 10000 for 10 Gbit/s. Configure the same value on all OSPF routers.

fields.label: Adjacency logging
fields.name: ospfLogAdjacency
fields.help: `Yes` generates `log-adjacency-changes`. `Detail` generates `log-adjacency-changes detail` and records additional OSPF adjacency state changes.

fields.label: Networks: Network|Wildcard|Area
fields.name: ospfNetList
fields.help: Enables OSPF on interfaces whose addresses match the configured networks. Area 0 is the backbone. Interfaces on the same link must use the same area.

fields.label: Interfaces: Interface|Area
fields.name: ospfInterfaceAreaList
fields.help: Interface-based OSPF activation. One line per interface: Interface|Area. Example: GigabitEthernet1/0/48|0 generates `ip ospf <process-id> area 0` under the interface.

fields.label: Passive-interface default
fields.name: ospfPassiveDefault
fields.help: passive-interface default prevents unintended OSPF adjacencies. Add no passive-interface only for the interfaces that should form neighbors.

fields.label: Default-information originate
fields.name: ospfDefaultOriginate
fields.help: Yes generates default-information originate. Always generates default-information originate always and advertises the default route even when no local 0.0.0.0/0 route exists.

fields.label: Non-passive interfaces
fields.name: ospfNoPassiveList
fields.help: Interfaces that are allowed to form OSPF neighbor adjacencies.

fields.label: Stub areas
fields.name: ospfStubAreas
fields.help: Generates area X stub no-summary.

fields.label: Interface|Cost|Priority|Network type
fields.name: ospfIfSettings
fields.help: Optional interface-specific OSPF parameters.

fields.label: IS-IS tag
fields.name: isisTag
fields.help: Local process name. Interfaces are assigned to this IS-IS process later. The tag does not need to be identical across the network.

fields.label: NET
fields.name: isisNet
fields.help: Network Entity Title identifying the IS-IS router. It must be unique. Example: 49.0001.0000.0000.0001.00.

fields.label: Interfaces: Interface
fields.name: isisIfList
fields.help: IS-IS is enabled per interface. Enter one interface name per line, for example GigabitEthernet0/0.

fields.label: BGP AS
fields.name: bgpAs
fields.help: Autonomous-System-number the local Routers. Gleiches AS in the neighbors = iBGP, anderes AS = eBGP.

fields.label: Router ID
fields.name: bgpRid
fields.help: Unique BGP router ID. It should remain stable; a loopback address is commonly used. It is important for BGP identification and troubleshooting.

fields.label: Neighbors: Neighbor IP|Remote AS|Optional description
fields.name: bgpNeighborList
fields.help: BGP does not discover neighbors automatically. Each neighbor must be configured explicitly with its IP address and remote AS number.

fields.label: Networks: network|mask
fields.name: bgpNetworkList
fields.help: BGP advertises only networks that exist in the routing table with a matching prefix. Format: network|mask.

fields.label: Advanced neighbor options: Neighbor|Update source|Multihop|Next-hop self|Password|Maximum prefixes|Soft reconfiguration inbound|Default originate
fields.name: bgpExtNeighborList
fields.help: Optional per-neighbor extensions. Each entry must match a neighbor from the neighbor list above.

fields.label: HSRP: Interface|Group|VIP|Priority|Preempt
fields.name: hsrpList
fields.help: HSRP provides a virtual gateway IP address. Clients use the VIP as their default gateway. The higher priority wins, and preempt allows the router to resume the active role after recovery.

fields.label: VRF: name|RD|description
fields.name: vrfList
fields.help: Generates VRF definitions.

fields.label: Interface|VRF|IP|mask
fields.name: vrfInterfaceList
fields.help: Assigns interfaces to a VRF. Caution: ip vrf forwarding removes an existing IP address on real devices.

fields.label: VRF|target|mask|Next-Hop
fields.name: vrfRouteList
fields.help: static VRF-routes.

fields.label: PrefixList: name|Seq|permit/deny|Prefix
fields.name: prefixListAdv
fields.help: Prefix lists used for routing-policy filters.

fields.label: RouteMap: name|permit/deny|Seq|PrefixList|Set-command
fields.name: routeMapList
fields.help: Route map with an optional prefix-list match and set command.

fields.label: Distribute list: Routing process|ACL/prefix list|In/out
fields.name: distributeList
fields.help: Creates a basic distribute-list assignment.

fields.label: SLA-ID|target|Source-Interface|Frequency|Track-ID
fields.name: ipSlaList
fields.help: Generates ICMP IP SLA with Track.

fields.label: target|mask|PrimaryNH|TrackID|BackupNH
fields.name: trackedRouteList
fields.help: Generates a tracked primary route plus a floating backup route.

fields.label: Area type: Area|stub/totally-stubby/NSSA/totally-NSSA
fields.name: ospfAreaTypeList
fields.help: Configures OSPF area types. The selected area type must match on all routers participating in the area.

fields.label: Authentication: Interface|MD5/clear|Key
fields.name: ospfAuthList
fields.help: Configures OSPF interface authentication. For MD5, the generator enables message-digest authentication and creates a message-digest key. All neighbors on the segment must use matching authentication.

fields.label: Timers: Interface|Hello|Dead
fields.name: ospfTimerList
fields.help: Configures OSPF hello and dead intervals on an interface. Neighboring routers on the same segment must use matching timer values.

fields.label: Summary: Area|Network|Mask
fields.name: ospfSummaryList
fields.help: Creates an OSPF area range on an ABR. Use a valid summary network and mask that cover the intended component routes.

fields.label: Neighbor policy: Neighbor|Route map in|Route map out|Prefix list in|Prefix list out|Keepalive|Hold
fields.name: bgpPolicyNeighborList
fields.help: Applies inbound and outbound route maps or prefix lists to an IPv4 BGP neighbor and optionally configures keepalive and hold timers.

fields.label: IPv6 address family: Neighbor|Remote AS|Prefix|Route map in|Route map out
fields.name: bgpAfIpv6List
fields.help: Creates an IPv6 unicast BGP address family, activates the IPv6 neighbor, advertises the specified prefix, and optionally applies inbound or outbound route maps.

fields.label: Enable IP CEF
fields.name: mplsCef
fields.help: Enables Cisco Express Forwarding, which is a prerequisite for common MPLS forwarding designs.

fields.label: Enable MPLS globally
fields.name: mplsGlobal
fields.help: Enables global MPLS forwarding where required by the target platform. MPLS must still be enabled on the intended core or transit interfaces.

fields.label: Label protocol
fields.name: mplsLabelProtocol
fields.help: Selects the label distribution protocol. LDP is the common choice for IOS and IOS-XE MPLS networks.

fields.label: LDP router ID interface
fields.name: mplsLdpRouterId
fields.help: Selects a stable LDP router ID. A loopback interface reachable through the IGP is normally recommended.

fields.label: Force router ID
fields.name: mplsLdpRouterIdForce
fields.help: Forces the selected LDP router ID. Existing LDP sessions may be rebuilt, so use this option deliberately.

fields.label: MPLS interfaces: Interface|Optional description|Optional MPLS MTU|LDP-IGP sync yes/no
fields.name: mplsInterfaceList
fields.help: Enables MPLS on core or transit interfaces. An MPLS MTU and LDP-IGP synchronization can be configured when required by the platform and design.

fields.label: LDP neighbor password: Neighbor IP|Password
fields.name: mplsLdpPasswordList
fields.help: Configures optional LDP neighbor authentication. The peer must use the same password.

fields.label: Optional MP-BGP AS number
fields.name: mplsBgpAs
fields.help: Local BGP autonomous system used for MPLS L3VPN and VPNv4. Leave empty when VPNv4 MP-BGP is not required.

fields.label: Optional VPNv4 update source
fields.name: mplsVpnv4UpdateSource
fields.help: Update source for VPNv4 peers. A loopback interface is normally used to keep the session independent of a physical link.

fields.label: VPNv4 peers: Peer IP|Remote AS|Optional description|Send extended community yes/no
fields.name: mplsVpnv4Peers
fields.help: Creates MP-BGP VPNv4 peers. Sending extended communities is normally required to transport route targets.

fields.label: L3VPN VRFs: VRF|RD|Import RT|Export RT|Optional description
fields.name: mplsVpnVrfList
fields.help: Creates MPLS L3VPN VRF definitions with a route distinguisher and import/export route targets. Duplicate VRF definitions are reported by validation.

fields.label: VRF: Name|RD|Description
fields.name: vrfDefList
fields.help: One line per VRF. The name is referenced by interfaces, routes, and routing protocols. The route distinguisher makes routes unique when identical networks exist in multiple VRFs. Description is optional.

fields.label: SVI in VRF: VLAN|VRF|IPv4|Mask|Reserved|Name|IPv6/prefix|IPv6 enable
fields.name: vrfSviList
fields.help: Creates an SVI inside a VRF. The reserved column remains empty for compatibility with older templates. Optional IPv6 addressing and ipv6 enable can be added to the interface.

fields.label: IPv4: VRF|Destination|Mask|Next hop
fields.name: vrfStaticList
fields.help: Creates an IPv4 static route in the selected VRF. The next hop must be reachable within that VRF.

fields.label: IPv6: VRF|Destination/prefix|Next hop
fields.name: vrfIpv6StaticList
fields.help: Creates an IPv6 static route in the selected VRF. A link-local next hop may also require an outgoing interface on the target platform.

fields.label: OSPFv2: VRF|Process|Network|Wildcard|Area|Router ID
fields.name: vrfOspfList
fields.help: Creates an OSPFv2 process for a VRF, assigns a unique router ID, and advertises the specified IPv4 network in the selected area.

fields.label: Process: VRF|Process|Reserved|Router ID
fields.name: vrfOspfv3List
fields.help: Creates an OSPFv3 IPv6 address family for a VRF. The reserved column remains empty for compatibility. OSPFv3 still requires a router ID in IPv4 notation.

fields.label: Interface: Interface|VRF|Process|Area
fields.name: vrfOspfv3IfList
fields.help: Enables the selected OSPFv3 process and area on an interface assigned to the VRF. The VRF column is used for documentation and validation.

fields.label: IPv4: VRF|AS|Neighbor|Remote AS|Network|Mask
fields.name: vrfBgpIpv4List
fields.help: Creates an IPv4 BGP address family for a VRF, configures the neighbor and remote AS, and advertises the specified network when it exists in the routing table.

fields.label: IPv6: VRF|AS|Neighbor|Remote AS|Prefix
fields.name: vrfBgpIpv6List
fields.help: Creates an IPv6 BGP address family for a VRF, activates the IPv6 neighbor, and advertises the specified IPv6 prefix.

fields.label: IPv6 interfaces: Interface|IPv6/prefix|Optional link-local address
fields.name: ipv6IfList
fields.help: IPv6 address with prefix, for example 2001:db8:10::1/64. A link-local address can be set manually, which is particularly relevant for static routes through fe80:: addresses.

fields.label: Basic pools: Pool|Network|Mask|Gateway|DNS
fields.name: dhcpPoolList
fields.help: Basic DHCP pool. Use the extended pools below when a domain, lease time, or option 150 is required.

fields.label: Excluded addresses: Start IP|Optional end IP
fields.name: dhcpExcludedList
fields.help: Addresses or ranges that the DHCP server must not assign.

fields.label: Extended pools: Pool|Network|Mask|Gateway|DNS|Domain|Lease|Option 150
fields.name: dhcpExtPoolList
fields.help: Extended DHCP pool with domain, lease time, and option 150 for VoIP or TFTP.

fields.label: ACL: Name|Permit/Deny|Protocol|Source|Destination
fields.name: aclList
fields.help: Extended ACL. Source and destination use IOS syntax. For example, destination 'any eq 22' permits SSH. ACLs filter traffic based on direction and interface.

fields.label: Assignment: Interface|ACL|In/Out
fields.name: aclApplyList
fields.help: Assigns an ACL to an interface. `in` filters traffic entering the interface; `out` filters traffic leaving the interface.

fields.label: Standard ACL: Number/Name|Permit/Deny|Source
fields.name: stdAclList
fields.help: A standard ACL filters only by source. It is commonly used for VTY or management access control.

fields.label: Extended ACL assistant: Name|Permit/Deny|Protocol|Source|Destination
fields.name: extAclList
fields.help: An extended ACL filters by source, destination, protocol, and optionally a port.

fields.label: Assistant assignment: Interface|ACL|In/Out
fields.name: aclAssignExtList
fields.help: Assigns ACLs created by the assistant to interfaces.

fields.label: IPv6 static routes: Destination/Prefix|Next hop/Interface
fields.name: ipv6StaticRoutes
fields.help: Creates static IPv6 routes, including routes using a link-local next hop and an outgoing interface.

fields.label: IPv6 ACL: Name|Permit/Deny|Protocol|Source|Destination
fields.name: ipv6AclList
fields.help: IPv6 ACL rules.

fields.label: IPv6 ACL assignment: Interface|ACL|In/Out
fields.name: ipv6AclApplyList
fields.help: Assigns an IPv6 ACL to an interface.

fields.label: RA suppression interfaces
fields.name: ipv6RaSuppressIfs
fields.help: Suppresses router advertisements on the listed interfaces.

fields.label: DHCPv6 relay: Interface|Destination
fields.name: dhcpv6RelayList
fields.help: Configures a DHCPv6 relay destination per interface.

fields.label: OSPFv3 process
fields.name: ospfv3Process
fields.help: OSPFv3 process ID.

fields.label: Router ID
fields.name: ospfv3Rid
fields.help: Router ID used by OSPFv3.

fields.label: Interface|Area
fields.name: ospfv3IfList
fields.help: Enables OSPFv3 IPv6 on Interfaces.

fields.label: BGP IPv6: AS|Neighbor|Remote AS|Prefix|Reserved|Update source|Outbound route map
fields.name: ipv6BgpList
fields.help: Configures IPv6 unicast BGP without a VRF, activates the neighbor, advertises the prefix, and optionally sets an update source and outbound route map.

fields.label: EIGRP IPv6: AS|Router ID|Interface
fields.name: ipv6EigrpList
fields.help: Configures EIGRP for IPv6 and enables it on the selected interface. Support depends on the IOS or IOS-XE platform.

fields.label: IS-IS IPv6: Tag|Interface
fields.name: ipv6IsisList
fields.help: Enables the IPv6 address family for an IS-IS process and activates IS-IS on the selected interface.

fields.label: ACL name
fields.name: aclAssistName
fields.help: Name of the extended IPv4 ACL, for example MGMT-SSH.

fields.label: Action
fields.name: aclAssistAction
fields.help: permit allows matching traffic; deny blocks matching traffic.

fields.label: Protocol
fields.name: aclAssistProtocol
fields.help: Protocol matched by the ACL rule. Port values are meaningful only for TCP or UDP.

fields.label: Source type
fields.name: aclAssistSrcType
fields.help: any matches every source. host matches one IP address. network matches an address and wildcard mask.

fields.label: Source value
fields.name: aclAssistSrc
fields.help: For host, enter one IP address. For network, enter the network and wildcard mask. Leave empty for any.

fields.label: Destination type
fields.name: aclAssistDstType
fields.help: any matches every destination. host matches one destination IP. network matches a destination network and wildcard mask.

fields.label: Destination value
fields.name: aclAssistDst
fields.help: For host, enter one IP address. For network, enter the network and wildcard mask. Leave empty for any.

fields.label: Port
fields.name: aclAssistPort
fields.help: Adds eq <port> to the destination portion of the rule. Applies only to TCP or UDP.

fields.label: Apply to interface
fields.name: aclAssistInterface
fields.help: Optional. Applies the generated ACL to this interface with ip access-group.

fields.label: Direction
fields.name: aclAssistDirection
fields.help: in filters traffic entering the interface; out filters traffic leaving the interface.

fields.label: no service pad
fields.name: hardNoPad
fields.help: Disables PAD Service.

fields.label: no ip source-route
fields.name: hardNoSourceRoute
fields.help: Disables IP Source Routing.

fields.label: no ip bootp server
fields.name: hardNoBootp
fields.help: Disables BOOTP Server.

fields.label: CDP active
fields.name: hardCdp
fields.help: No generates no cdp run.

fields.label: LLDP active
fields.name: hardLldp
fields.help: Yes generates lldp run.

fields.label: Unused interface range
fields.name: hardUnusedRange
fields.help: Optional interface range that should be disabled as unused, for example Gi0/10-24.

fields.label: NAT Typ
fields.name: natMode
fields.help: Selects which NAT type is exported. This prevents multiple NAT variants from being generated accidentally. Use All only when multiple NAT methods are intentionally combined.

fields.label: NAT interfaces: Interface|Inside/outside
fields.name: natIfList
fields.help: inside = internes network, outside = direction WAN/Internet. NAT requires korrekt markierte Interfaces.

fields.label: PAT overload: ACL|Outside interface
fields.name: natOverloadList
fields.help: PAT/NAT overload translates many internal source addresses to the outside interface address.

fields.label: Static NAT: Inside local|Inside global
fields.name: natStaticList
fields.help: 1:1 NAT between interner and globaler address.

fields.label: Portforward: protocol|InsideLocal|InsidePort|InsideGlobal|GlobalPort
fields.name: natPortList
fields.help: Static PAT / port forwarding.

fields.label: NAT Pool: name|StartIP|EndIP|mask|ACL
fields.name: natPoolList
fields.help: Dynamischer NAT Pool with ACL.

fields.label: NAT exemption: Route map|ACL|Interface
fields.name: natExemptList
fields.help: Foundation for NAT exemptions, for example VPN traffic.

fields.label: GRE: TunnelID|IP|mask|Source|Destination
fields.name: greList
fields.help: GRE creates an unencrypted tunnel between two endpoints. Combine GRE with IPsec when encryption is required.

fields.label: One Cisco command per line
fields.name: customConfigList
fields.help: For commands that do not yet have a dedicated module. They are copied to the export exactly as entered. Caution: this field is not syntax-validated.

fields.label: TunnelID|Tunnel-IP|mask|Source|Destination|PSK|ACL|CryptoMap|TransformSet
fields.name: greIpsecList
fields.help: Generates the foundation for GRE over IPsec with an ISAKMP policy, transform set, crypto map, and tunnel interface.

fields.label: Primary WAN interface
fields.name: wanPrimaryIf
fields.help: Primary WAN interface.

fields.label: Backup WAN interface
fields.name: wanBackupIf
fields.help: Backup WAN interface.

fields.label: Primary next hop
fields.name: wanPrimaryNh
fields.help: Primary next hop.

fields.label: Backup next hop
fields.name: wanBackupNh
fields.help: Backup next hop.

fields.label: Track target
fields.name: wanTrackTarget
fields.help: Destination monitored by IP SLA tracking.

fields.label: Zones, one per line
fields.name: zoneList
fields.help: Security zones.

fields.label: Interface|Zone
fields.name: zoneMemberList
fields.help: Assigns interfaces to security zones.

fields.label: Class|Protocol|Policy|Action (inspect/pass/drop)
fields.name: zonePolicyList
fields.help: Generates Class-Map and Policy-Map.

fields.label: Zone pair|Source zone|Destination zone|Policy
fields.name: zonePairList
fields.help: Generates Zone-Pair with Service-Policy.

fields.label: Interface|IP|Mask|Server IP
fields.name: dmzList
fields.help: Generates a basic DMZ interface and ACL foundation.

fields.label: Peer|PSK|Outside interface|Crypto ACL|Transform set|Crypto map
fields.name: ipsecS2SList
fields.help: Site-to-site IPsec without GRE, based on IKEv1.
